name: IR Remote Control for Appliance Automation
description: Infrared remote control system for appliance automation with learning capabilities
author: ATHENA Team

irReceiverPin.title: IR Receiver Pin
irReceiverPin.description: Digital pin connected to IR receiver

enableLearning.title: Enable IR Learning
enableLearning.description: Enable learning mode for new remote controls

metadata.description: Wiring diagram for IR receiver and relay module

metadata.content:
# IR Remote Control for Appliance Automation

## Overview
This template implements an infrared remote control system for appliance automation with learning capabilities. It can learn multiple remote controls and execute commands to control up to 4 relays for various appliances.

## Features
- IR remote learning capability
- Support for multiple remotes (up to 10)
- 4-channel relay control
- Command storage in EEPROM
- Auto-off timers for safety
- Status LED indication
- Audio feedback via buzzer
- Serial command interface
- Command toggle and set modes
- Auto-off protection

## Learning Mode
- **Activation**: Hold any button for 3 seconds
- **Timeout**: 10 seconds
- **LED Indicator**: Fast blinking during learning
- **Audio Feedback**: Confirmation beeps
- **Storage**: Commands saved to EEPROM

## Supported Remote Types
- **TV Remotes**: Standard television remotes
- **AC Remotes**: Air conditioner remotes
- **Universal Remotes**: Most universal remotes
- **Custom Remotes: Any IR remote

## Command Types
- **Toggle**: Turn relay on/off (default)
- **Set**: Set relay to specific state
- **Auto-Off**: Turn off after specified delay
- **Multi-Relay**: Control multiple relays simultaneously

## Configuration Options
- **IR Receiver Pin**: Digital pin for IR receiver (default: 11)
- **Relay Pins**: Digital pins for 4 relays (default: 2,3,4,5)
- **Status LED Pin**: LED for status indication (default: 13)
- **Buzzer Pin**: Audio feedback (0=disabled, default: 0)
- **Learning Mode**: Enable/disable learning capability (default: true)
- **Auto-Off Delay**: Default auto-off delay in minutes (0=disabled, default: 0)
- **Device Name**: Custom device name (default: "IR Remote Controller")
- **Remote Names**: Names for learned remotes

## EEPROM Storage
- **Remote Information**: Remote hashes and names
- **Command Data**: Command hashes and relay masks
- **Auto-Off Timers**: Command-specific timers
- **Configuration**: System settings

## Wiring
- IR Receiver VCC → Arduino 5V
- IR Receiver GND → Arduino GND
- IR Receiver Data → Arduino Digital Pin {{.irReceiverPin}}
- Relay Module VCC → Arduino 5V
- Relay Module GND → Arduino GND
- Relay Module IN1 → Arduino Digital Pin {{.relay1Pin}}
- Relay Module IN2 → Arduino Digital Pin {{.relay2Pin}}
- Relay Module IN3 → Arduino Digital Pin {{.relay3Pin}}
- Relay Module IN4 → Arduino Digital Pin {{.relay4Pin}}
- LED Anode → Arduino Digital Pin {{.ledPin}} (with 220Ω resistor)
- LED Cathode → Arduino GND
- Buzzer + → Arduino Digital Pin {{.buzzerPin}} (if enabled)
- Buzzer - → Arduino GND (if enabled)

## Appliance Connections
- **Relay COM**: Power supply positive terminal
- **Relay NO**: Appliance positive terminal
- **Appliance Negative**: Power supply negative terminal

**Power Requirements**:
- **Relay Rating**: Match appliance requirements
- **Power Supply**: Adequate for connected appliances
- **Wire Gauge**: Appropriate for current draw


## Serial Commands
Send commands via serial monitor:
```
HELP    - Show help information
LEARN   - Enter learning mode
EXIT    - Exit learning mode
STATUS  - Show system status
CLEAR   - Turn off all relays
```

## Command Storage
Each learned command stores:
- **Hash**: Unique identifier for the IR signal
- **Relay Mask**: Bitmask for which relays to control
- **Toggle**: Whether to toggle or set state
- **Auto-Off**: Auto-off delay in minutes

## API Integration
The system can be extended with:
- MQTT integration for IoT platforms
- Web interface for remote control
- Mobile app integration
- Voice assistant integration
- Automation rules and scheduling

## Dependencies
- IRremote library v3.3.0
- ArduinoJson v6.21.3
- EEPROM library (built-in)

## Troubleshooting
- **IR Not Receiving**: Check IR receiver connections
- **Commands Not Working**: Verify remote compatibility
- **Relays Not Responding**: Check relay wiring and power supply
- **EEPROM Issues**: Verify EEPROM size and connections
- **Learning Problems**: Check timing and button press duration
- **False Triggers**: Check IR interference sources

## Advanced Features
- **Multi-Command**: Execute multiple commands with one button
- **Scene Control**: Predefined appliance combinations
- **Voice Control**: Integration with voice assistants
- **Mobile App**: Remote control via smartphone
- **Cloud Sync**: Backup and sync configurations
- **Analytics**: Usage statistics and patterns

## Safety Features
- **Auto-Off Protection**: Prevents appliances from being left on
- **Learning Mode Protection**: Prevents accidental learning
- **Power Monitoring**: Check for overload conditions
- **Error Handling**: Robust error detection and recovery
- **Status Indication**: Clear visual and audio feedback

## Performance Specifications
- **IR Range**: 5-10 meters (typical)
- **Response Time**: <100ms
- **Memory Usage**: ~2KB for configuration
- **EEPROM Size**: 512 bytes
- **Relay Switching**: <10ms
- **Power Consumption**: ~50mA (without relays)

## Supported IR Protocols
- **NEC**: Most common TV remotes
- **RC-5**: Some older remotes
- **RC-6**: Some air conditioner remotes
- **Sony SIRC**: Sony protocol
- **RCMM**: Some universal remotes
- **RC-5**: Some newer protocols

## Compatibility
- **TV Remotes**: Most brands (Samsung, LG, Sony, etc.)
- **AC Remotes**: Most brands (Carrier, Trane, etc.)
- **Universal Remotes**: One-for-all remotes
- **Custom Remotes: Any IR remote

## Maintenance
- **IR Receiver Cleaning**: Keep IR receiver clean for optimal range
- **Battery Check**: Replace remote batteries periodically
- **Connection Check**: Verify all wiring connections
- **EEPROM Health**: Check for data integrity
- **Pattern Updates**: Update command sets as needed
- **Firmware Updates**: Keep firmware current

## Use Cases
- **Home Automation**: Control lights, fans, and other appliances
- **Home Theater**: Control TV, sound system, and devices
- **Climate Control**: Air conditioners and heaters
- **Lighting Control**: Room and accent lighting
- **Appliance Management**: Turn devices on/off remotely
- **Accessibility**: Voice control for accessibility needs
- **Energy Management**: Automated energy saving
